articles.title_en: Russia’s largest online marketplace, Wildberries, starts compensating sellers for goods destroyed in Ukrainian drone attacks. Sellers say it’s not enough.
articles.lead_en: Wildberries, Russia’s largest online marketplace, has announced it will compensate sellers for goods lost in Ukrainian drone attacks. On Wednesday evening, July 22, marketplace owner Tatyana Kim said that sellers whose goods were damaged in Elektrostal, in the Moscow region, would receive payments “within 24 hours.” She said the company would prioritize “supporting the smallest and least protected entrepreneurs,” adding that more than 88,000 sellers fall into that category.
